prompt: A NebulaForge Creator Nebula tool that turns offline inspiration fragments into a navigable 3D star cloud for creators, planners, and writers, running as a Svelte + TypeScript single-page app with an immersive WebGL/Three.js canvas at its core. Users enter through a local-only profile screen using a username plus a device-local password (8–20 characters and must include at least 1 number); this gate is purely a convenience on the current browser device (not a security boundary) and supports an optional “cooldown” screen after repeated failures to discourage typos without claiming real protection. After entry, users can bulk import up to 1,000 inspiration cards from CSV or JSON via drag-and-drop or file picker, with row-level validation and inline error review in a Table: title up to 30 characters, body up to 500 characters, date formatted as YYYY-MM-DD, mood from 1–5, and up to 5 tags. Imported cards render as an interactive star map where tag groups form “galaxies,” mood drives star color, and date controls orbital distance; the scene includes camera orbit/pan/zoom controls, adjustable lighting presets, and object picking so clicking a star opens a Modal with full details and an Edit flow. Full-text search works entirely offline with filters (tag, mood range, date window) and sortable results that can be highlighted back onto the star field. A “Voyage Mission” tracker counts a day as completed when the user views at least 10 distinct cards; 7 consecutive days unlock a new stardust visual effect, and any missed day resets the streak to 0. All data stays on-device: preferences (theme, navigation layout, footer text, carousel images, language selection) persist in LocalStorage, while cards, thumbnails, import files, parsing rules, and search indexes persist in IndexedDB. Heavy tasks such as indexing, batch imports, and effect precomputation run in a Web Worker with a visible progress Drawer and cancellable queue; job health (last run time, throughput, failures) is shown in an in-app Monitor tab with downloadable error logs, and threshold “alerts” appear as Toast notifications only (no email/SMS/webhooks). To support extensibility without any network dependency, the app exposes a local “Star Map SDK” (layer control, feature query/highlight, drawing and measurement tools) plus an OpenAPI-style spec and sample code for embedding the canvas into other offline pages. A multi-source parsing module can extract cards from user-provided offline HTML/JSON snapshots using CSS selectors/XPath/JSONPath with rule versioning and canary testing against a small sample set before applying to the full file. Backups export as a single downloadable file (optionally passphrase-protected for privacy, without promising tamper-proof security), and a Service Worker caches app assets for installable, fully offline PWA use; multi-tab coordination uses BroadcastChannel to warn about simultaneous edits and refresh views consistently.
